title: polemonium caeruleum subsp. vulgare
intro: Polemonium caeruleum subsp. vulgare, also known as common Jacob's ladder, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Polemoniaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Polemonium caeruleum subsp. vulgare include its potential to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can be dried or used fresh in various remedies.
